name: UNLV capitalizes on Las Vegas's sports boom with a new bachelor's degree program
summary: UNLV's William F. Harrah College of Hospitality is introducing the state's first undergraduate degree in sport hospitality management, the college announced Monday.The bachelor's degree program will begin in the fall.

name: ‘Democrats for Lombardo’ might say more about the Dems on the list than Lombardo, expert says
summary: Republican Gov. Joe Lombardo has the support of a group of Nevada Democrats in his race against Attorney General Aaron Ford, a Democrat, the governor’s campaign announced Monday.

name: Primm avoids ghost town fears with Terrible's operation deal
summary: Primm, a small oasis community on the California state line, is no longer facing the prospect of becoming a modern-day ghost town after a deal was finalized to shift several local properties to Terrible’s.

name: FOX5 Investigates: Nevada data center boom strains energy, water, and land resources
summary: State memo says northern Nevada could become the second-largest data market in the world, but infrastructure questions remain.

name: New heat law takes effect in Nevada, meant to address heat deaths and illnesses
summary: The Vegas Valley was experiencing extreme heat on Monday, leading Clark County to activate its cooling stations around town.

name: UNLV now offering degree in sport hospitality management
summary: A release from the University of Nevada, Las Vegas said that the new degree hopes to fulfill the workforce demand for sports management professionals.

name: After Tragedy, a Network of Universities Takes on Healing Together
summary: When violent incidents shatter campuses, universities look to those who have endured similar tragedies for guidance on helping students move forward.

name: iGaming trends and predictions for 2026
summary: AI is one of the main iGaming industry trends in 2026, but adoption is uneven. A report published by the University of Nevada, Las Vegas (UNLV) International Gaming Institute and KPMG found that 81.5% of gambling companies use generative AI, 66.7% use conversational AI, and 60.5% use predictive AI (2026).

name: Shoppers keep spending even as inflation hits the pump
summary: American consumers have remained relatively resilient against high gas prices as a result of the war in Iran, according to David Tinsley, managing director and senior economist at the Bank of America Institute.
